result.summary: networksecurity.googleapis.com exposes a Google Cloud Network Security REST API plus access via the gcloud CLI; no GraphQL or MCP surface was found.
result.description: Google Cloud Network Security provides managed network-security resources in Google Cloud, such as policies, endpoint groups, and related firewall and inspection configuration. The service is part of Google Cloud’s networking and security product set.

gcp_oauth_access_token.label: Google Cloud OAuth 2.0 access token
gcp_oauth_access_token.setup: Authenticate to Google Cloud using one of the methods in [Authenticate for using REST](https://cloud.google.com/docs/authentication/rest), then obtain an access token with the `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/cloud-platform` scope. For local developer use, the easiest path is usually the [gcloud CLI authentication flow](https://cloud.google.com/sdk/docs/authenticate) and then using `gcloud auth print-access-token`.

gcp_api_key.label: Google Cloud API key
gcp_api_key.setup: Create an API key in Google Cloud as described in [Manage API keys](https://cloud.google.com/docs/authentication/api-keys). The discovery document indicates requests can include the `key` query parameter as an alternative to an OAuth token, but individual Network Security API methods also declare the `cloud-platform` OAuth scope, so API-key-only access may be limited in practice.
